200.q: What house is Luna Lovegood in?
What is the name of Hagrid's giant dog?
What is Hagrid's three-headed dog called?
What sport is played on broomsticks?
Who is the headmaster of Hogwarts?
What platform does the Hogwarts Express leave from?
Who is Harry's enemy at school?
What is the name of the villain?
Who plays Harry Potter in the films?
What is the name of Hagrid's pet dragon?
What is the magical map called?
What house is known for cunning and ambition?

200.a: Lights up the tip of your wand
A fellow Gryffindor who kills Nagini with the Sword of Gryffindor
An object containing a fragment of a dark wizard's soul
A small winged ball — catching it ends the Quidditch match and scores 150 points
A hidden wizarding shopping street in London
Magical creatures bound to serve wizarding families — Dobby is the most famous
The Dark Lord — born Tom Marvolo Riddle — who killed Harry's parents
Keeper of Keys and Grounds at Hogwarts — Harry's first connection to the wizarding world
Summons an object to the caster — a Summoning Charm
A popular wizarding drink served at the Three Broomsticks — slightly warming
A magical hat that places new students into their Hogwarts house
Deputy Headmistress, Transfiguration teacher, and head of Gryffindor house
A dark, dangerous forest on Hogwarts grounds — home to centaurs, unicorns, and giant spiders
A potion temporarily transforming the drinker into another person's physical appearance

200.q: What is the Chamber of Secrets?
200.a: A hidden chamber in Hogwarts created by Salazar Slytherin — home to the Basilisk
Peter Pettigrew (Wormtail) — hiding in Animagus form for 12 years

200.q: What does a Boggart transform into?

400.q: Who teaches Potions after Snape moves to Defense Against the Dark Arts?
400.a: Horace Slughorn

400.q: What is the violent tree on the Hogwarts grounds called?
400.a: The Whomping Willow

400.q: What creature can only be seen by people who have witnessed death?
400.a: Thestrals
Crookshanks

400.q: What is the hidden map used by Harry and his friends?
400.a: The Marauder's Map

400.q: What map shows everyone at Hogwarts?
400.a: Marauder's Map

400.q: What giant spider belongs to Hagrid?
400.a: Aragog

400.q: What broom model does Harry receive from McGonagall?
400.a: Nimbus 2000

400.q: What map reveals secret passages at Hogwarts?
400.a: Marauder's Map
Hogsmeade

400.q: What creature guards the Chamber of Secrets?
400.a: Basilisk
An object containing a piece of a soul

400.q: How many Horcruxes did Voldemort make?
400.a: 7 (intentionally 6 + Harry)

400.q: What is the name of the Weasley's house?
400.a: The Burrow

400.q: What are the three Deathly Hallows?
400.a: Elder Wand, Resurrection Stone, Cloak of Invisibility

400.q: What is the name of Voldemort's snake?
400.a: Nagini

400.q: What magical creature guards Gringotts vaults?
400.a: Dragons
Snape

400.q: What is the Patronus charm used for?
400.a: Repelling Dementors

400.q: What form does Harry's Patronus take?
400.a: A stag

400.q: Who is the gamekeeper at Hogwarts?
400.a: Rubeus Hagrid
The Elder Wand, the Resurrection Stone, and the Invisibility Cloak
A phoenix feather from Dumbledore's phoenix Fawkes
Severus Snape

400.q: Who are the four founders of Hogwarts?
400.a: Godric Gryffindor, Salazar Slytherin, Rowena Ravenclaw, Helga Hufflepuff
The language of snakes — a rare and dark-associated ability
James Potter (Prongs), Sirius Black (Padfoot), Remus Lupin (Moony), Peter Pettigrew (Wormtail)
A Muggle has no magic; a Squib is born to a magical family but has no powers
Voldemort's most loyal follower — cousin of Sirius Black

400.q: What is the Room of Requirement?
400.a: A magical room that appears when needed, providing whatever is required
A house-elf who becomes fiercely loyal to Harry and is freed
A stone basin used to review memories — Dumbledore uses one with Harry
Avada Kedavra (death), Crucio (torture), Imperio (mind control)

400.q: What is the Order of the Phoenix?
400.a: A secret organisation founded by Dumbledore to fight Voldemort and the Death Eaters
Neither can live while the other survives — linking Harry and Voldemort's fates
Alastor Moody — a retired Auror famous for his magical eye and constant vigilance
Draco dormiens nunquam titillandus — Never tickle a sleeping dragon
A positive force conjured to repel Dementors — takes the shape of an animal

400.q: What is the Sword of Gryffindor?
400.a: A goblin-made sword that imbibes what makes it stronger — used to destroy Horcruxes
An eccentric Ravenclaw who becomes one of Harry's closest friends
A magical map of Hogwarts showing every person's location in real time
Voldemort's symbol — a skull with a serpent tongue — cast when Death Eaters kill
The fraudulent DADA teacher in Year 2 — takes credit for others' achievements
A Metamorphmagus Auror and Order of the Phoenix member — she marries Remus Lupin
A Hogwarts course studying magical properties of numbers — Hermione's favourite subject
Dementors — soul-sucking creatures that feed on happiness
DADA teacher in Year 1 — secretly hosting Voldemort's spirit on the back of his head

400.q: What is the Mirror of Erised?
400.a: A mirror showing the viewer's deepest desire — Erised is 'desire' spelled backwards
A real historical alchemist — in HP he created the only known Philosopher's Stone
Magically defending the mind against external penetration — opposite of Legilimency

400.q: What is the significance of the Elder Wand's allegiance?
400.a: The wand's true master is whoever disarmed the previous master — Draco unknowingly became master, then Harry

600.q: What magical branch studies predicting the future?
What does the spell Sectumsempra do?
600.a: It slashes the target as if by an invisible sword

600.q: What is Voldemort's middle name?
What is Snape's mother's maiden name?
What Gringotts vault temporarily held the Philosopher's Stone?
What is the motto of the Black family?
What spell unlocks doors?
What is Dumbledore's phoenix called?
What charm summons objects?
What helps Harry breathe underwater in the Triwizard Tournament?
What is the room that appears when needed called?
What object stores Slughorn's altered memory?
What spell destroys a Horcrux with a fang in the Chamber?
What dragon does Harry face in the first Triwizard task?
What station does Harry return to after death in Deathly Hallows Part 2?
What shop does Ollivander own in Diagon Alley?
What is the core of Harry's wand?
What phoenix provides feathers for Harry's and Voldemort's wands?
What spell kills?
Who was the original owner of the Elder Wand (in legend)?
What is the Room of Requirement?
600.a: A room that appears when someone needs it

600.q: Who is R.A.B.?
What did Snape's Patronus reveal?
600.a: A doe — his love for Lily Potter

600.q: What is Hermione's middle name?
What is the name of Neville's parents?
Who destroys the last Horcrux (Nagini)?
What does Felix Felicis do?
What year was the Battle of Hogwarts?
Who is the true master of the Elder Wand at the end?
What is Voldemort's real name?
Who became Minister for Magic after the war?
List all Horcruxes and how they were destroyed.
600.a: Diary: Basilisk fang. Ring: Sword. Locket: Ron/Sword. Cup: Hermione/Basilisk fang. Diadem: Fiendfyre. Nagini: Neville/Sword. Harry: Voldemort's own Killing Curse

600.q: What is the full prophecy linking Harry and Voldemort?
600.a: The one with the power to vanquish the Dark Lord approaches... neither can live while the other survives

600.q: Original owner of the Elder Wand before Dumbledore?
600.a: Gellert Grindelwald (who took it from Gregorovitch)

600.q: What is the Fidelius Charm?
600.a: It conceals a secret inside a person's soul — only the Secret-Keeper can reveal it

600.q: Why is Harry being a Horcrux significant?
600.a: Voldemort accidentally made Harry a Horcrux — Harry had to let Voldemort kill him to destroy that soul fragment

600.q: Dumbledore's full name?
Why did the Sorting Hat consider putting Harry in Slytherin?
600.a: He had Slytherin qualities — partly because he carried a piece of Voldemort's soul

600.q: What is the significance of Lily Potter's sacrifice?
600.a: Her love-based sacrifice created magical protection that deflected Voldemort's Killing Curse

600.q: What is the Animagus transformation?
600.a: A wizard who can transform into an animal — James (stag), Sirius (dog), Peter (rat), McGonagall (cat)

600.q: What is the nature of Voldemort's soul at the end?
600.a: So fragmented from creating Horcruxes, his soul becomes a stunted, damaged, baby-like form seen in King's Cross

600.q: Why could Neville Longbottom have been the Chosen One?
600.a: The prophecy fit him too — but Voldemort chose Harry, marking him as his equal

600.q: What is the wizarding economy?
600.a: Based on Galleons (gold), Sickles (silver), and Knuts (bronze) — Gringotts is the goblins' bank

600.q: Who is Credence Barebone?
600.a: An Obscurial in Fantastic Beasts — later controversially revealed as Aurelius Dumbledore

600.q: What is wandlore?
600.a: Wands choose the wizard — made from wood and a magical core, each combination creates unique properties

600.q: What is the Triwizard Tournament?
600.a: A dangerous competition between Hogwarts, Beauxbatons, and Durmstrang — minimum age 17

600.q: What is the difference between Harry's wand and Voldemort's?
600.a: Both have cores from the same phoenix (Fawkes) — causing Priori Incantatem when they duel

600.q: What is the significance of the Time-Turner in Prisoner of Azkaban?
600.a: Harry and Hermione use it to go back three hours — saving Buckbeak and Sirius Black

600.q: What is Dumbledore's backstory with Grindelwald?
600.a: He was once close friends and shared Grindelwald's vision of wizard supremacy — until Ariana's death changed him

600.q: What is the Deathly Hallows symbol?
600.a: A triangle (cloak) enclosing a circle (stone) with a line (wand) — also used by Grindelwald as his mark

600.q: What is the significance of Snape's always?
600.a: Snape's Patronus was always a doe — the same as Lily Potter's — revealing his lifelong love for her
